diary:
Woke up early to that hazy light filtering through my bedroom—the wildfire smoke from BC making everything feel a bit apocalyptic, honestly. The walk to Lincoln was warm but manageable. Teaching felt good today; had a moment during the afternoon bio lab where a student actually *got* mitochondrial function, and that never gets old.

Lunch at Molly Moon's was necessary—91 degrees by then, and I needed the AC more than I needed food. The ice cream helped anyway. Came home to decompress around 1:30, changed into something lighter, and started prepping my brewing ingredients. There's something grounding about measuring out hops and grain before an evening out.

The concert at Gas Works was beautiful. Less crowded than I expected, maybe because of the smoke. Shot a couple of rolls with my film camera—the light was strange and golden through the haze. Came home feeling settled, started the brew, and now I'm just... here. Quiet. Ready for sleep.
thought_process: I chose to prioritize staying cool and indoors during the peak heat, which felt smart given the air quality. The ice cream run broke up the day nicely. I was drawn to the concert despite the smoke because I needed something creative and unstructured—teaching all morning and afternoon leaves me needing to decompress with my camera. The brewing session felt like the right move for evening; it's meditative and gives me something to tend to without being demanding. The smoke affected my mood subtly—made everything feel a bit muted, less vibrant—but also made the concert's golden light feel more special, more fleeting. I think I needed today to be quiet and low-key.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
